title: Hercules's Big Day
tagline: He did something completely right, on purpose, without anyone's help. Nobody can figure out how. Including Hercules.
arc_note: HERCULES FOCUS EPISODE — He saves a small village from a flooding river, correctly, without breaking anything, using his strength exactly right. The comedy: he cannot explain how he knew what to do. The lesson: sometimes you've been absorbing information all along without realizing it. Athena's scrolls have been read by him. Upside-down, over her shoulder, while pretending to look at something else.

scenes.title: The River
scenes.narration: A river near Olympus had been flooding its banks — farmland at risk, a small village downstream. The group had arrived to help. The plan was Athena's: temporary stone barrier at the northern bend, redirect the flow. She explained it. Everyone nodded. Then Baby Hercules walked directly to the river, looked at it for twelve seconds, and moved three specific boulders in a specific order that perfectly redirected the water. In four minutes. The flood stopped. Everyone stared.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Flooding river scene — farmland threatened. Athena with a blueprint. Baby Hercules walking past everyone, looking at the river, then moving three specific boulders in sequence — each one calculated, not random. Water redirecting perfectly. He stands back. The flood stopped. The group stares at him. 16:9.

scenes.title: How Did You Know
scenes.narration: Athena asked how he knew which boulders. He said: 'The water went there.' She said: 'But the sequence — you had to know the pressure differential at the northern bend.' He said: 'The water went there first.' She said: 'That's not an explanation.' He looked at the river. He said: 'It looked like your scroll. The water pattern. The one you showed Zeus in the library.' She said: 'I showed Zeus that in the library.' He said: 'I was behind you.' She said: 'You were facing the wrong way.' He said: 'I read it upside down.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena interrogating Hercules, getting increasingly specific answers that he can't articulate but are correct. The reveal: he read her water-flow scroll upside-down over her shoulder while pretending to look elsewhere. She stares at him. Her expression: recalibrating everything she knows about Hercules. 16:9.

scenes.title: What He's Been Absorbing
scenes.narration: Athena sat down with Hercules and asked him — very carefully — what else he'd read upside-down. The list was long. He had absorbed: half of her water-flow dynamics scroll, a large portion of her bridge engineering notes, an entire diagram about lever mechanics (which explained why his accidental improvements to the columns in EP015 were, in fact, correct), and three pages from her section on mountain geology. He had understood all of it. He had just never been asked.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena going through a list with Hercules, increasingly astonished. He lists things he absorbed — bridge notes, lever mechanics, mountain geology. Each one accompanied by a small flashback of Hercules nearby, seemingly doing something else, actually absorbing information. Her expression: he's been learning this whole time. He just didn't know it was learning. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Village Thanks Them
scenes.narration: The village offered a feast. At the feast, the village elder thanked each team member. When she got to Hercules, she said: 'The young one with the lion cape — he knew exactly what he was doing.' Zeus said: 'He did!' Athena said: 'He really did.' Perseus said: 'That's been happening for a while, I think.' Hercules, eating his third bowl of everything, said: 'I'm good at water.' He was. He hadn't known it until today.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Village feast — warm, grateful, celebratory. Village elder thanking each of them. When she gets to Hercules, everyone agrees enthusiastically. Hercules with three bowls of food, looking pleased and slightly puzzled by the praise, saying 'I'm good at water.' He is. He is genuinely good at water. 16:9.

scenes.title: Athena Opens a New Section
scenes.narration: On the way home, Athena opened a new section in her scroll. She titled it: HERCULES — APPLIED ENGINEERING. She wrote the date, the river, the boulder sequence, and a note: Has been absorbing technical information via adjacent observation for at least eighteen months. Application: excellent. Knowledge of source material: confirmed. She added a second note: Consider giving him the actual scrolls. He looked over her shoulder as she wrote. He said: 'That's a good idea.' She said: 'You're reading upside down again.' He said: 'Habit.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena writing in her scroll, new section titled HERCULES — APPLIED ENGINEERING. Hercules reading over her shoulder upside-down, commenting on her 'give him the scrolls' note. She catches him. He just smiles. 16:9.

scenes.title: He Gets the Scrolls
scenes.narration: She gave him three scrolls the next morning. Water dynamics, lever mechanics, structural load-bearing. He took them very carefully — more carefully than she'd expected. He rolled them correctly. He put them in a protective case he'd made from his spare lion cape fabric. He said: 'I'll be careful with them.' She said: 'I know.' He beamed. He had never had scrolls before. He'd been learning without them for eighteen months. He was going to find out what happened with actual scrolls.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena handing Hercules three scrolls — more careful than expected, rolling them correctly, storing them in a hand-made protective case. His beam when she says 'I know.' The group watching this small ceremony. Something has been recognized. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: Learning happens more ways than anyone plans for. Hercules learned upside-down, out of the corner of his eye, while pretending to look at something else. And it worked. It stuck. It came out perfectly when it was needed. Some people just absorb things differently. The wise thing is to recognize it and hand them the scrolls. See you next time on Little Olympus!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — Hercules on the Olympus steps with his three scrolls, studying them. Not upside-down this time — right way up, genuinely reading. The river in the background, stable and flowing correctly. Athena watching with a small smile. 16:9.

lesson: People learn differently. Some absorb by watching, by overhearing, by proximity. Recognizing how someone actually learns is as important as what you're teaching.
youtube_title: Baby Hercules Did Something PERFECTLY Right (He Can't Explain How) 😂 | Little Olympus Episode 16
next_episode_preview: Episode 17 — Zeus Takes It Seriously! He's been entering every event and winning nothing. This episode he decides he wants to be actually good at one thing.
